name: d3
summary: The most powerful data visualization library for the web — bind data to SVG/Canvas elements and transform them with scales, axes, and layouts.

story.problem: You need a chart that no charting library supports, or you want complete control over every pixel of your visualization. Libraries like Chart.js or Recharts are constrained — d3 gives you the raw building blocks.
story.mental_model: d3 is not a charting library — it's a DOM manipulation + data transformation toolkit. The core pattern: select elements, bind data (.data()), then enter/update/exit the joined selection. Built-in: scales (map data domain → visual range), axes, shape generators, and force/tree/hierarchy layouts.
story.when_to_use: Custom visualizations that no library supports, journalism/scrollytelling data stories, network graphs, geo maps, animated transitions between data states, or when you need to own every detail.
story.when_not_to_use: Standard bar/line/pie charts in a product (use Recharts or Chart.js — they're 10x faster to implement). d3's learning curve is steep.

alternatives.name: recharts
alternatives.reason: React chart library built on d3, much easier for standard charts

alternatives.name: chart.js
alternatives.reason: Canvas-based, easy to use, standard chart types

alternatives.name: observable-plot
alternatives.reason: From d3 creator, high-level grammar-of-graphics API

nodes.label: d3
nodes.summary: Umbrella package re-exporting all d3 submodules

nodes.label: d3.select() / selectAll()
nodes.summary: Select DOM elements, returns d3 selection

nodes.label: Data Join
nodes.summary: .data() binds array to selection, .enter()/.exit() handle add/remove

nodes.label: d3.scaleLinear() etc.
nodes.summary: Map data domain to visual range (px, color, etc.)

nodes.label: d3.axisBottom() etc.
nodes.summary: Generate SVG axis from a scale

nodes.label: d3.line()
nodes.summary: Generate SVG path d attribute from data array

nodes.label: .transition()
nodes.summary: Animate changes smoothly with easing functions

nodes.label: d3.forceSimulation()
nodes.summary: Physics-based force layout for network graphs
